name: RenX Enterprises Corp.
business: RenX Enterprises Corp., through its subsidiaries Resource Group and Zimmer Equipment Inc., transforms organic green waste into engineered soil and mulch products, and provides waste logistics and collection services. The company operates in three segments: biomass recycling, logistics, and real estate (legacy).

exposures.sentence: Through our subsidiary, Zimmer Equipment Inc. ( ZEI ), we provide comprehensive waste logistics and collection services for our own products as well as for products of third parties through ZEI s owned fleet of high-capacity transportation equipment and third-party contractors engaged by us.
Resource Group, through its subsidiaries, is a vertically integrated, full-service operator in the engineered soils and organic recycling industry.

we are in the process of implementing the Microtec UTM 1200 Turbo Mill system at our Myakka City facility. The UTM 1200 is a high-efficiency milling and processing technology designed to enhance the throughput and output quality of our existing organics processing operations, including the production of engineered soils and mulch products. Phase 1 deployment is targeted for 2026 and is expected to meaningfully expand processing capacity at Myakka City.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 deployment of Microtec UTM 1200 Turbo Mill system
catalysts.sentence: Phase 1 deployment is targeted for 2026 and is expected to meaningfully expand processing capacity at Myakka City.

tamClaims.claim: The global organic waste management market was valued at approximately $17.4 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach approximately $37.9 billion by 2034, growing at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 8.1%.
tamClaims.sentence: The global organic waste management market was valued at approximately $17.4 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach approximately $37.9 billion by 2034, g growing at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 8.1% (Source: Precedence Research / market.us).

tamClaims.claim: The global compost market was valued at approximately $6.7 billion in 2025, with North America accounting for approximately 30% of global market share.
tamClaims.sentence: The global compost market was valued at approximately $6.7 billion in 2025, with North America accounting for approximately 30% of global market share (Source: Business Research Insights).

tamClaims.claim: The global soil amendments market, which includes compost, biosolids, and organic mulch products, was valued at approximately $19.6 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 11.2% through 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The global soil amendments market, which includes compost, biosolids, and organic mulch products, was valued at approximately $19.6 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 11.2% through 2030 (Source: Research and Markets), with recent U.S. tariff adjustments on imported amendment inputs creating additional competitive tailwinds for domestic producers of waste-derived substrates.

tamClaims.claim: The Florida freight and logistics market was valued at approximately $78.3 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach approximately $97.1 billion by 2030, growing at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 4.5%.
tamClaims.sentence: The Florida freight and logistics market was valued at approximately $78.3 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach approximately $97.1 billion by 2030, growing at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 4.5% (Source: Mordor Intelligence).
